response:
Sec. 1  
In 2019, the Chief Minister of Andhra Pradesh was Y. S. Jaganmohan Reddy. He was the leader of the YSR Congress Party. He served as the Chief Minister since 2014.  

***  
He was elected as the Chief Minister in the 2014 state elections. He was re-elected in the 2019 state elections. His party won a majority in the assembly.  

***  
He was born on September 25, 1970, in Kadapa district. He is the son of Y. S. Rajasekhar Reddy. He became the Chief Minister at the age of 44.  

***  
He is known for his development initiatives in the state. He focused on infrastructure and education. He also emphasized health and agriculture.  

***  
He introduced several welfare schemes for the people. He aimed to improve the quality of life. His policies were aimed at inclusive growth.  

***  
He faced criticism for some of his policies. Some people questioned his governance style. However, he remained popular among many.  

***  
He has a strong presence in the political landscape. His leadership has shaped the state's direction. His tenure is marked by both achievements and challenges.  

***  
He continues to be a prominent figure in Andhra Pradesh. His legacy is still being discussed. His impact on the state's politics remains significant.
